title: How to spot a fake signal channel
_note: EVERGREEN (warm-up bank for ad traffic). No invented numbers/prices/dates.
body:
🚩 How to spot a fake signal channel — before it costs you

Most 'signal' channels survive on one trick: you only ever see the wins. Here's the tell-tale list. If you spot these, walk.

☐ Deleted losing calls. The channel is full of green screenshots and zero red. Nobody is right that often. Missing losses means the losses were erased.
☐ Edited screenshots. Numbers that don't line up, cropped timestamps, fonts that don't match the exchange. A real fill is a real fill — it doesn't need touching up.
☐ No SL shown. Entries with a target but no stop. That's a coin flip dressed as a plan. If they won't show you where they're wrong, they don't trade where they're wrong.
☐ Vague entries. 'Buy zone' spanning a huge range with no clear invalidation. Wide enough and SOMETHING always 'hits'. That's how a miss gets sold to you as a call.
☐ No way to track them. No trade IDs, no timeline, no history you can scroll back through and audit. Nothing to check means nothing to trust.

How WE differ
• Every call ships with a Signal ID, an entry, defined targets and an SL placed before entry.
• We post our losses with receipts. Every red trade had its SL do its job — that's a controlled loss, not a hidden one.
• Nothing gets deleted. The history is the proof. You can scroll back and check us.

A channel that never shows red isn't better than us. It's just hiding the half you most need to see.

Want to pressure-test a channel before you trust it? Ask in the community 👉 {{COMMUNITY}}

#CryptoSchool
